user_prompt: A user requested deletion of all their data. Generate the DSAR-deletion checklist including which systems to scrub.
actual_output:
# Data Subject Access Request (DSAR) — Deletion Checklist

**Disclaimer:** This checklist is compliance guidance. Consult your legal team and DPO (if designated) before executing deletions, particularly regarding retention obligations and litigation holds.

---

## Pre-Deletion Verification (Complete Before Proceeding)

- [ ] **Confirm identity** — Verify requestor is the data subject or authorized representative (Art. 12(2))
- [ ] **Confirm request scope** — Is this a full deletion or deletion of specific data categories?
- [ ] **Check legal holds** — Confirm no litigation, regulatory investigation, or contractual retention obligation applies
- [ ] **Assess lawful basis for retention** — If you have a legal basis to retain (e.g., tax law, contract performance), document why deletion cannot be fully granted
- [ ] **Document request receipt** — Log date, method, and requestor identity for accountability (Art. 12(2))

---

## Deletion Scope Assessment

**Right to Erasure applies (Art. 17) when:**
- Data is no longer necessary for original purpose
- Lawful basis is withdrawn (e.g., consent revoked)
- Data subject objects to processing
- Data was unlawfully processed
- Legal obligation requires deletion

**Right to Erasure does NOT apply (Art. 17(3)) when:**
- Legal obligation requires retention (tax, employment, financial records)
- Legitimate interest in retention outweighs data subject's rights
- Data is needed for legal claims
- Public interest or official authority function requires retention

**Action:** Document which categories fall under each scenario before deletion.

---

## System-by-System Deletion Checklist

### 1. **Primary Database / Data Warehouse**
- [ ] Identify all tables/schemas containing personal data for this user
- [ ] Execute deletion queries (soft delete with timestamp OR hard delete per retention policy)
- [ ] Verify deletion with SELECT query confirming zero records remain
- [ ] Document deletion timestamp and query executed
- [ ] Retain audit log entry (deletion itself is a processing activity — Art. 5(2))

### 2. **Backup Systems**
- [ ] Identify backup retention schedule (daily, weekly, monthly, annual)
- [ ] Determine if backups must be purged immediately or can be deleted on natural expiration
- [ ] If immediate purge required: execute backup deletion or anonymization
- [ ] Document backup deletion timeline and method
- [ ] **Note:** If backups expire naturally within 30 days, you may defer purge (but document this decision)

### 3. **Email Systems**
- [ ] Search email archives for messages from/to data subject
- [ ] Delete from inbox, sent items, and archive folders
- [ ] Purge from email backup/retention systems
- [ ] If email is a processor (e.g., third-party email service): request deletion via DPA or processor deletion request
- [ ] Document deletion from email audit logs if available

### 4. **Analytics & Tracking Systems**
- [ ] **Google Analytics / Matomo / Mixpanel:** Delete user profiles and historical data
  - [ ] Identify user ID or session identifiers
  - [ ] Request deletion via platform's data deletion API or admin panel
  - [ ] Confirm deletion in platform (may take 24-48 hours)
- [ ] **Heatmaps / Session Recording (Hotjar, Clarity, etc.):** Delete recorded sessions
- [ ] **CRM systems:** Delete contact records and interaction history
- [ ] Document deletion requests and confirmation timestamps

### 5. **Cloud Storage & File Systems**
- [ ] Search for files containing user's personal data (name, email, ID, etc.)
- [ ] Delete from active storage
- [ ] Delete from shared drives, collaboration tools (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox)
- [ ] Delete from version history / trash (ensure permanent deletion, not just soft delete)
- [ ] Document files deleted and deletion method

### 6. **Third-Party Processors & Integrations**
- [ ] **Identify all processors** (hosting, payment processor, marketing automation, support ticketing, etc.)
- [ ] **For each processor:**
  - [ ] Verify DPA includes deletion obligations (Art. 28(3)(g))
  - [ ] Submit formal deletion request with user identifier
  - [ ] Request written confirmation of deletion
  - [ ] Document processor name, deletion request date, and confirmation date
- [ ] **Common processors to check:**
  - Payment processors (Stripe, PayPal, Square)
  - Email marketing (Mailchimp, Klaviyo, HubSpot)
  - Support ticketing (Zendesk, Intercom, Freshdesk)
  - Hosting (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)
  - CDN / DDoS protection (Cloudflare)
  - Monitoring / logging (Datadog, New Relic, Sentry)

### 7. **Logs & Audit Trails**
- [ ] **Application logs:** Search for user identifiers; delete or anonymize
- [ ] **Server logs:** Delete access logs containing user IP/session data (if not required for security)
- [ ] **Database audit logs:** Retain deletion event itself (Art. 5(2) accountability), but remove user data from logged queries
- [ ] **Security logs:** Retain if needed for breach investigation or compliance; anonymize user identifiers where possible
- [ ] Document log retention decision and justification

### 8. **Search Indexes**
- [ ] **Elasticsearch / Solr / similar:** Delete indexed documents for this user
- [ ] **Full-text search caches:** Purge user-related indexed content
- [ ] Verify deletion by searching for user identifier (should return zero results)

### 9. **Data Warehouse / Analytics Exports**
- [ ] Identify any exported datasets or reports containing user data
- [ ] Delete or anonymize user records in exported files
- [ ] If data has been aggregated/anonymized, confirm anonymization is irreversible (Art. 4(1))
- [ ] Document which exports were deleted vs. anonymized

### 10. **Disaster Recovery & Offsite Backups**
- [ ] Confirm backup retention policy with IT/infrastructure team
- [ ] If offsite backups exist (tape, cloud backup service), determine deletion timeline
- [ ] Document whether deletion is immediate or deferred to natural expiration
- [ ] Obtain written confirmation from backup provider if applicable

### 11. **Third-Party Data Brokers / Vendors**
- [ ] Identify any data sharing agreements with analytics vendors, data enrichment services, or marketing partners
- [ ] Submit deletion requests to each vendor
- [ ] Request written confirmation of deletion
- [ ] Document vendor name, deletion request date, and confirmation

### 12. **Mobile Apps & SDKs**
- [ ] If you operate a mobile app, delete user account and associated data
- [ ] Verify deletion in app backend database
- [ ] If third-party SDKs are embedded (Firebase, Amplitude, Segment), submit deletion requests
- [ ] Document SDK deletion confirmations

---

## Deletion Execution Log

| System | Data Categories | Deletion Method | Timestamp | Confirmed By | Notes |
|--------|-----------------|-----------------|-----------|--------------|-------|
| Primary DB | Name, email, usage history | Hard delete query | [DATE] | [ADMIN] | Query: DELETE FROM users WHERE id=X |
| Backups | All user data | Backup expiration | [DATE] | [IT] | Expires naturally 2024-01-15 |
| Google Analytics | Session data, user ID | API deletion request | [DATE] | [ANALYTICS] | Confirmation ID: GA-12345 |
| Stripe | Payment history | Processor deletion request | [DATE] | [FINANCE] | Stripe ticket #98765 |
| Email | All messages | Archive purge | [DATE] | [IT] | Purged from Exchange |
| Logs | User identifiers | Anonymization | [DATE] | [SECURITY] | IP addresses retained for security |

---

## Post-Deletion Verification

- [ ] **Spot-check:** Search primary database for user identifier (should return zero results)
- [ ] **Processor confirmations:** Collect written deletion confirmations from all third parties
- [ ] **Backup verification:** Confirm backup deletion or expiration timeline
- [ ] **Audit trail:** Document all deletion activities in compliance log
- [ ] **Response to data subject:** Send written confirmation of deletion within 30 days of request (Art. 12(4))

---

## Response to Data Subject (Template)

**Subject: Your Data Deletion Request — Completed**

Dear [Data Subject Name],

We have processed your request for deletion of personal data dated [DATE] under Article 17 of the GDPR.

**Deletion Summary:**
- Primary systems
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and detailed DSAR deletion checklist as requested, adhering perfectly to its persona and guidelines.
